title: silene scabriflora subsp. scabriflora
intro: Silene scabriflora subsp. scabriflora is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the rough-fruited catchfly. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Silene scabriflora subsp. scabriflora include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Silene scabriflora subsp. scabriflora is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
